question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the dual lookup path in getByEvmAddressAndType() — where zero-prefixed addresses use findById() and others use findByEvmAddress() — to determine whether an entity was registered with a native numeric ID versus a true EVM address, leaking entity registration metadata?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Base32 string with non-standard padding (e.g., missing '=' padding characters) to getByAliasAndType(), causing Apache Commons Codec Base32.decode() to silently produce a different byte array than the correctly padded version, potentially matching a different entity alias in the database?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Base32 string with extra padding characters (e.g., excessive '=' characters) to getByAliasAndType(), causing Apache Commons Codec Base32.decode() to silently ignore the extra padding and decode to the same bytes as a valid alias, bypassing any alias format validation?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getByIdAndType() uses reference equality (==) for EntityType comparison in the filter lambda 'e -> e.getType() == type', and if EntityType is not a true Java enum but a class with multiple instances, bypass the type check to retrieve entities of any type?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null EntityType to getByIdAndType(), causing the filter 'e -> e.getType() == type' to evaluate 'e.getType() == null', which returns false for all entities (since getType() returns a non-null enum), effectively making the method always return Optional.empty() and causing a silent denial of service for legitimate queries?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null EntityType to getByAliasAndType(), causing the filter 'e -> e.getType() == type' to always return false, making the method return Optional.empty() regardless of whether the alias exists, creating a boolean oracle that can be used to confirm alias existence by comparing null-type vs valid-type responses?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null EntityType to getByEvmAddressAndType(), causing the filter 'e -> e.getType() == type' to always return false, making the method return Optional.empty() regardless of whether the EVM address exists, creating a boolean oracle to confirm EVM address existence?
